headline: Maharashtra gets 20% more cooking gas on government order
facts: The central government has increased Maharashtra's commercial LPG quota by 20%. Hotels, restaurants, dhabas, food processing units, industrial canteens, and dairy businesses will get priority for the extra supply.
impact: Restaurants and small food businesses in Maharashtra will face less pressure on cooking gas costs and availability over the coming weeks.
left_lens: Government stepping in to support small businesses and the hospitality sector during economic strain, ensuring food businesses can operate without fuel shortages.
right_lens: Centre allocating resources efficiently to boost business activity in a major state, helping the commercial food industry remain competitive and productive.
public_pulse: Mixed sentiment — restaurant owners and small businesses welcome the relief, while some question whether the increase is substantial enough to meaningfully lower operating costs.
original_title: Commercial LPG quota in Maharashtra raised by 20% on Centre’s directive

headline: AI autocomplete might be making our writing less original
facts: Research suggests that predictive text tools—the kind that finish your sentences—could reduce how creatively people write. A study titled 'Predictive Text Encourages Predictive Writing' found a direct link between using these tools and less unique writing styles.
impact: Your texts and social media posts might start sounding more generic if you rely on autocomplete suggestions.
left_lens: Tech companies are eroding human creativity and individuality by pushing addictive predictive tools that make people lazy writers. This threatens authentic human expression and cultural diversity.
right_lens: Predictive text is a useful tool that saves time and helps people communicate faster. Users can choose whether to accept suggestions, so it's ultimately about personal choice and convenience.
public_pulse: Young people are split—some find autocomplete helpful for quick responses, others worry their writing feels fake when they use it too much. Many don't think about the impact until someone points it out.
original_title: With AI finishing your sentences, what will happen to your unique voice on the page?

headline: Indian woman became first female fellow at Glasgow medical college
facts: Jamini Sen became the first woman Fellow of Glasgow's Royal College of Physicians in 1912. The institution, founded in 1599, had never admitted women to this status before.
impact: Women in medicine got a major confidence boost from seeing one of their own break into prestigious institutions that had kept them out for centuries.
left_lens: Progressive achievement showing how women fought systemic barriers in male-dominated fields. Highlights the erasure of women's contributions to history and celebrates those who refused to accept exclusion.
right_lens: Recognition of individual merit and achievement. A woman earned a position at one of the world's oldest medical colleges through her own excellence, not special treatment.
public_pulse: Modest curiosity with appreciation for historical figures who challenged norms. Many find it surprising that this story remains relatively unknown despite her groundbreaking achievement.
original_title: The forgotten Indian woman trailblazer in British medicine
